title: Hieracium apoleptum
taxonomy: Hieracium apoleptum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is a member of the Hieracium genus, which includes about 1000 species of plants. The plant's classification also includes its order, Asterales, and class, Magnoliopsida. This classification places Hieracium apoleptum within the larger plant kingdom, Plantae. Its specific classification is a result of ongoing research and taxonomic revisions in the field of botany.
uses: Hieracium apoleptum is a lesser-known species within the hawkweed genus, and research on its specific medicinal properties is limited. However, many species within the Hieracium genus have been traditionally used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Some studies suggest that extracts from these plants may have antioxidant and wound-healing effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Hieracium apoleptum. More extensive studies on its specific properties and applications are required to confirm its efficacy.

parts: Hieracium apoleptum, also known as Devil's-bit scabious, is a plant species native to parts of Europe. The medicinal parts of Hieracium apoleptum include the leaves, flowers, and roots. The leaves and stems are rich in alkaloids and flavonoids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. side_effects: Hieracium apoleptum is a plant that is known to be toxic to humans and animals. When ingested, it can cause gastrointestinal symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The plant's sap can also cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. In severe cases, ingestion of Hieracium apoleptum can lead to more serious health issues, including kidney damage and respiratory problems. It is essential to avoid touching or consuming the plant to prevent any potential side effects.
